mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% annually signals central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL's predictive power fails in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10 in regression), or 5d returns in triggered regimes show negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST) combined with increasing real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-RSAFS signal fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05), or 60d returns in triggered regimes show negative or flat performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity, reducing input costs and improving corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO's predictive power collapses (Rank IC < 0.03), or 20d SPX returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO trends.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth (GDPC1) signals underperforming aggregate demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns (p > 0.10 in regression), or 60d SPX returns show positive correlation with GDP declines.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) raises borrowing costs, dampening equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns despite FEDFUNDS increases, or FEDFUNDS's predictive power fails in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances with UNRATE < 4%, or UNRATE's predictive power fails (Rank IC < 0.03).
